nodes.label: AI as springboard for pedagogical and research ideas
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable source requiring constant fact-checking
nodes.grounding_span: I used the model more as an initial springboard for my thinking

nodes.label: AI as low-level writing assistant (proofreading, polishing)
nodes.grounding_span: I find the model very useful at rewriting sentences and paragraphs

nodes.label: AI as memory aid for half-remembered references
nodes.label_negative: AI as hallucinator of false references
nodes.grounding_span: asking for a half-remembered book that was about narrative in novels

nodes.label: AI as time-saving tool for administrative tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI as ethically suspect or secretive tool
nodes.grounding_span: If a model helps us do our job, faster and with less work, than everyone is for it

nodes.label: Openness about AI use in academia
nodes.label_negative: Secrecy about AI use due to stigma
nodes.grounding_span: I want to have conversations about how it is used, and how we might use it better. But I also feel I should shut up and use it without telling others

nodes.label: Pedagogical effectiveness and student engagement
nodes.grounding_span: suggestions about where to engage with students, and where to present new information

nodes.label: Research productivity and discovery
nodes.grounding_span: occasionally giving me things I would never have thought of

nodes.label: Professional efficiency and workload management
nodes.grounding_span: If a model helps us do our job, faster and with less work

nodes.label: Intellectual honesty and institutional integrity
nodes.grounding_span: never contravening university policy (particularly the letter of its law)

nodes.label: Pragmatic acceptance of AI despite inaccuracies
Frustration with AI hallucination and need for fact-checking
nodes.grounding_span: it would get elements of the plot wrong, or--critically--falsify quotes or even events

nodes.label: Pride in ability to adapt AI effectively
nodes.grounding_span: if anything I feel clever, that I can adapt it to do what I want

nodes.label: Reluctance to advocate for AI use among resistant colleagues
nodes.grounding_span: I do think it would be annoying to have to introduce its uses to colleagues who are less close to the technology than I am

nodes.label: Desire for nuanced institutional AI policies
nodes.grounding_span: A more granular sense of the categories of AIs' uses might help fewer people move from using LLMs on the DL to openly talking about their uses

nodes.label: Iterative verification and manual fact-checking
nodes.grounding_span: I have an etext of the novel in front of me, and I always just check manually

nodes.label: Strategic compartmentalization of AI use based on task autonomy
nodes.grounding_span: The less autonomy I give the model, the better it does
